question.text: What is the cooling period before a company can undertake another buyback after the expiry of the previous buyback period?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI Buyback Regulations, no company shall make any offer of buyback within a period of one year reckoned from the date of expiry of the buyback period of the preceding offer of buyback. **The Trap:** 6 months sounds plausible as many SEBI regulations use 6-month periods (e.g., lock-in periods), but the buyback cooling period is specifically 1 year. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) may reference buyback timelines in the context of corporate action adjustments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Plan buyback timing carefully — the 1-year cooling period means a company gets at most one buyback opportunity per year.
